vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start
vBRIEFInfo.description: Auto-synthesized minimal plan for PAN-1107: Ship role cost $4.95 for a single sync-main merge on PAN-457 — investigate cost spike

plan.title: Ship role cost $4.95 for a single sync-main merge on PAN-457 — investigate cost spike
plan.author: panopticon-cli/auto-start

narratives.Problem:
## Symptom

\`agent-pan-457-ship\` ran for 23m and accumulated \$4.95 in gpt-5.5 spend resolving 19 sync-main merge conflicts on PAN-457 (\`feature/pan-457\`).

That's an order of magnitude higher than the expected ship-role cost. Ship is meant to be mechanical conflict resolution + verification on a cheap workhorse model; previous ship runs typically cost a few cents.

Status line at completion:
\`\`\`
gpt-5.5 (gpt-5.5)  ctx 63%  189/200.0k  out 260  cost \$4.9465  +2/-1
\`\`\`

## Contributing factors (suspected)

- 19 conflicts is on the high end. A few of them involved \`.beads/config.yaml\` (with conflict markers inside YAML) and \`.pan/spec.vbrief.json\`, which the ship agent had to repeatedly re-scan.
- One tool call referenced a stale path (\`src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/MissionControl/styles/mission-control.module.css\`) that no longer exists. That suggests the agent was using cached state from training or earlier turns rather than reading the actual tree.
- Verification ran the full \`npm test\` (329 files) which is appropriate but pulls heavy context.
- Ship apparently ran through several rounds of \"stage → verify → fix\" loops based on output budget (out=260).

## Why it matters

Sync-main is invoked automatically on every \`pan start\` for an existing workspace. If sync-main routinely produces 19-conflict merges that cost \$5 in ship time, the per-restart cost adds up fast.

## Acceptance

- Capture per-attempt cost in the ship role audit log so spikes are visible from the dashboard
- Investigate whether the agent's repeated re-scans of conflict files can be short-circuited (e.g., feed it the conflict-marker locations directly instead of re-reading every file)
- Decide whether ship should bail out and ask for human help past a certain cost/conflict-count threshold rather than burn through a workhorse budget
narratives.Proposal: Implement the issue directly from the tracker-provided title and body.
